title: Extend an existing HB run by additional MCMC iterations
summary: Extend an existing HB run by additional_iterations more MCMC iterations on top of its persisted chain. Useful when an initial `fit` run did not converge: instead of re-running from scratch, picks up where the chain left off and samples more iterations. Running `fit` for 10 000 iterations and then `fit-extend` for another 5 000 produces the same posterior as a single 15 000-iteration run. If the original fit recorded a holdout (test_input), the holdout evaluation is recomputed against the larger draw pool when the run completes.
description: Resumes a completed (or interrupted) HB MCMC run from its persisted chain and samples `additional_iterations` more iterations on top of it. The kernel re-uses the existing `spec.json` so all hyperparameters, sign constraints, segment count, threads, and adaptive step size settings carry forward unchanged — only the iteration count and (optionally) the burnin fraction can be changed. The result after extension is mathematically equivalent to having originally run for `(original_iterations + additional_iterations)` iterations in one go.
usage:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::fit-extend"]]
run_dir = "<existing run dir>"
run_name = "hbmh_run"
additional_iterations = <int>
# optional:
burnin = 0.2

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Directory of an existing HB run. Must contain `spec.json`, `manifest.postcard`, `checkpoint.postcard`, and `status.postcard` — the bookkeeping files written by `fit` that allow the chain to resume from the last saved state. The chain chunks under `chunks/` are appended to, not replaced.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: additional_iterations
arguments.description: Number of additional MCMC iterations to sample on top of the existing chain. Must be at least 1. After extension, the chain length is `original_iterations + additional_iterations` and any subsequent `fit-extend` call will resume from this new endpoint.

arguments.name: burnin
arguments.description: Burnin fraction used when re-evaluating the holdout after the extended run completes. Only relevant if the original fit recorded a `test_input`. Default `0.2`. The burnin is applied to the *full* extended chain, not to the new iterations alone.

details:
`fit-extend` exists because HB chains often need more iterations than the user's first guess. The naive alternative is to re-run `fit` with a larger iteration budget, but that throws away the warm-up work and the adaptively-tuned step size from the original run. Extending picks up exactly where the chain left off and is therefore much cheaper.

The command does not accept hyperparameter overrides on purpose: if you change the prior, the segment count, the sign constraints, etc., you no longer have a single coherent chain — you have two pieces of two different chains glued together, and the resulting posterior is meaningless. To explore a different specification, run a fresh `fit` instead.

When the original run recorded a holdout, the holdout log-likelihood is recomputed at the end of the extension against the new (larger) post-burnin draw pool. To re-evaluate with a different burnin without further extending, just call `finalize` with that burnin.

The complementary cross-validation entry point is `cv-fit-extend`, which extends every fold of a `cv-fit` run in the same way.
value:
On success, the existing `run_dir` is updated in place: new chain chunks are appended under `chunks/`, `status.postcard` and `manifest.postcard` are updated to reflect the new chain length, and the `exports/` summary is regenerated against the larger draw pool.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, periodic `event.run.progress` events, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: Extending preserves the original hyperparameters and adaptive step size — to change those, run a fresh `fit` instead.
Extending is mathematically equivalent to having originally run for `(original + additional)` iterations: the chain state is fully restored from the checkpoint.
If you only want to re-evaluate with a different burnin and don't need more iterations, use `finalize` instead — it doesn't sample anything.

see_also.description: Initial fit — run this first; extend if it undershoots.
Cross-validation counterpart — extends every fold of a `cv-fit` run.
Re-summarise an existing chain with a different burnin.

examples.title: Extend a run by 5 000 more iterations
Extend and re-evaluate the holdout with a higher burnin
Short diagnostic extension to check if a struggling chain recovers

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Directory of an existing HB run (must contain spec.json + manifest.postcard + checkpoint.postcard + status.postcard).

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: additional_iterations
fields.description: Number of additional MCMC iterations to sample on top of the existing chain.

fields.name: burnin
fields.description: Burnin fraction used when re-evaluating the holdout after the extended run completes (only relevant if the original fit recorded a test_input). Default: 0.2.

artifacts.title: Updated model summary
artifacts.description: Segment-level mean partworths and mixture probabilities recomputed from the extended chain. Compare against the pre-extension summary to see whether additional draws changed the preference estimates meaningfully.

fields.name: segment_means
fields.description: Mean partworth vector for each population segment.

fields.name: mixture_probabilities
fields.description: Estimated share of respondents in each segment.

artifacts.title: Per-respondent partworths (updated)
artifacts.description: Individual-level preference scores recomputed from the larger draw pool. These are the estimates to use for market simulations and downstream analysis.

fields.name: <attribute_level_columns>
fields.description: Estimated partworth for each attribute level.
